data.title: Nebius (NBIS) Stock Down after Q4 Earnings — Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Nebius Group (NBIS) stock trended lower in early Friday trading after the AI cloud infrastructure provider reported fourth-quarter results that missed revenue estimates, even though sales were higher than a year earlier. TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data also points to continued investor caution toward the stock, adding to the pressure on the stock.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Key Takeaways from Nebius' Q4

Nebius reported revenue of about $227.7 million for the quarter, sharply higher than a year ago but below expectations. Losses of $173 million also widened from the year-ago quarter as the company increased spending to expand its data center capacity.

Management said demand for its AI computing services remains strong. The company ended the year with about 170 megawatts of active power and plans to reach 800 megawatts to 1 gigawatt of connected capacity by the end of 2026. Nebius also reported annual recurring revenue of around $1.25 billion and posted a positive adjusted EBITDA quarter, showing higher usage of its platform.

However, investors appeared focused on costs and execution. Operating expenses climbed significantly as the company invested in new data center infrastructure, and the business is still far from profitability.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data shows caution among retail investors. Based on activity from more than 852,000 tracked portfolios, investors' sentiment toward Nebius is currently Negative.

About 2.3% of tracked portfolios hold NBIS shares, with investors allocating an average of 4.69% of their portfolios to the stock. However, recent activity shows investors have been reducing exposure. The number of portfolios holding NBIS rose slightly over the past seven days but declined about 1.8% over the last 30 days.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Wobble Ahead of Key CPI Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed early Friday as traders anticipate the key consumer price index (CPI) data this morning. Economists forecast inflation rising 2.5% year-over-year and 0.3% from the previous month. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.13% and 0.02%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.13% at 6:10 a.m. EST on February 13.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, all three indexes closed lower amid growing fears that AI is slowly taking over other sectors such as real estate, trucking, and software, after hitting software and finance stocks earlier in the week. The S&P 500 fell 1.6%, the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2%, and the DJIA slipped 1.3%.

After markets closed, Applied Materials (AMAT) jumped 11% after reporting strong earnings and guidance. Airbnb (ABNB) gained around 2% on upbeat outlook comments, while Pinterest (PINS) slid 14% following weak quarterly results and a soft forecast. Moreover, Apple (AAPL) dropped 5%, recording its biggest one-day decline since April 2025, on news that it may delay its planned AI Siri upgrade.

On the earnings front, notable companies including Moderna (MRNA) and Wendy's (WEN) will report earnings today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.12%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.96 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,973 per ounce on Friday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flurry of mixed corporate earnings.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on February 13, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.26%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.41%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 1.21%, and the Topix decreased 1.63%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AMD Stock Faces an AI Reality Check, Says Top Analyst
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is often viewed as Nvidia's (NVDA) main rival in artificial-intelligence chips. However, one Wall Street analyst said AMD still trails Nvidia in the AI accelerator market. Top DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria initiated coverage of AMD with a Neutral rating and a $220 price target, implying only about 3% upside from current levels.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

His caution is not about AMD's overall business. The company remains strong in consumer and server CPUs. Instead, his concern is AMD's role in the fast-growing market for AI accelerators.

Why the Analyst Is Cautious

AMD's Instinct GPUs, including the MI300 series, look competitive on paper. However, DA Davidson analyst Gil Luria described the company as "a marginal AI accelerator player… playing catch-up."

His concern is not the chip itself but how AI systems run at scale. Training modern models requires thousands of processors working together, which makes networking, software, and system integration just as important as raw speed. Nvidia currently has an advantage in that full setup, while AMD is still building its ecosystem.

Luria noted that the chips may "benchmark great on paper," but "customers are not buying spec sheets." What matters is how efficiently large clusters operate. When many processors are linked together, performance depends on the connections between them, an area where Nvidia still "has a hold," while AMD "doesn't have a serious answer," according to the analyst.

He pointed to OpenAI as an example. The lab needs enormous computing power and is not tied to its own chips, yet it still appears to rely more on Nvidia platforms and other custom solutions for its largest workloads. Companies running the biggest AI models tend to choose systems that already work reliably at scale.

Overall, the analyst's Neutral rating reflects uncertainty about how quickly AMD can expand its role in large AI deployments.

Is AMD a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMD stock based on 24 Buys, eight Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMD price target of $289.10 per share implies 40.38% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: NUE, STLD, RS: U.S. Steel Stocks Slide as Trump Weighs Tariff Rollback
data.description:
Shares of several U.S. metal producers fell in pre-market trading Friday after reports that President Donald Trump's administration may scale back certain tariffs on steel and aluminum goods.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The shares of the following companies fell as of 7:17 a.m. EST:

  • Nucor (NUE) — 3.62% to $181.97
  • Steel Dynamics (STLD) — 5.64% to $188.26
  • Reliance Steel (RS) — 1.75% to $350.01
  • Cleveland-Cliffs (CLF) — 2.51% to $10.49
  • ArcelorMittal (MT) — 2.29% to $60.18

Trump Eyes Tariff Ease for Steel and Aluminum

According to the Financial Times, administration officials are worried about the impact of recent tariff hikes on consumer prices for products such as food-and-drink cans and pie tins. However, the deliberation comes as the U.S. midterm elections scheduled for November draw near, with the cost of living at the top of the agenda.

In June, the Trump administration raised duties on imported steel and aluminum products to 50% globally, excluding the UK. In the following month, the scope of these tariffs was expanded to more than 400 derivative products, including appliances, motorcycles, wind turbines, mobile cranes, railcars, and electric vehicle parts.

A Boost for Metals Producers?

Depending on the scale of the rollback, access to cheaper imported steel and aluminum products could make it harder for domestic producers to pass on costs to final consumers, thereby putting pressure on their margins.

Reports have noted that parts of the arrangement being considered include exempting some goods from the tariffs and suspending the inclusion of new categories of goods on the list. 

The Trump administration has deployed tariffs as a key part of its international trade policy, with higher levies targeted at critical industries such as semiconductors and pharmaceuticals. Last month, the administration rolled out a 25% tariff on "certain advanced AI chips" but announced a one-year delay to the tariff raise on furniture, kitchen cabinets, and vanities.
